element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Foundational threat; game-naming element establishes core loop
Players learn weapon pickup and ammunition management before facing specialized enemy types
Werewolves take reduced hits from silverware; vampires die faster to crucifixes - teaches strategic weapon selection
Sustain mechanic; various potions provide speed boost or temporary monster transformation
Required win condition; at least one neighbor must be saved per level; all killed neighbors block progression
Visual novelty and thematic monster clustering; different locales host appropriate enemy types
Tourist neighbors transform into unrescuable werewolves at nightfall; introduces time-pressure element

element_introduction_order.element: Advanced weapons (flamethrower, ice pops, bazookas, weed-whackers)
element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Specialized tools for late-stage enemies; higher ammo scarcity
Key collection creates exploration pause points and encourages thoroughness

element_introduction_order.element: Dr. Tongue's castle / final boss encounter
element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Narrative payoff; endgame challenge that consolidates all learned mechanics

typical_relief_triggers: Clearing all enemies in a space
Saving a neighbor (progress toward exit door)
typical_tension_triggers: Multiple enemy types in confined space
rhythm_pattern.pacing_descriptor: Wave-based arena combat interspersed with exploration. Tension escalates as neighbor count drops toward critical threshold. Day-night cycles introduce sudden difficulty spikes on affected levels. Weapon scarcity and enemy type variety create pacing variety across 48 levels plus 7 optional bonus stages.

spatial_grammar.optional_content_signal: Bonus levels (7 optional stages among 48 total); optional weapon caches and isolated neighbor clusters reward exploration
spatial_grammar.secret_convention: Hidden weapons (flamethrower concealed inside wall; paint can pickup normally inaccessible); developers included unused level palette (snowy palette for Zombie Panic) suggesting hidden content design patterns

difficulty_curve.early_game: Zombie Panic and early suburban levels introduce basic zombie enemies and foundational weapon/neighbor mechanics in relatively open areas. Safety emphasis establishes player agency and comfort.
difficulty_curve.mid_game: Shopping malls and themed environments introduce specialized enemy types (vampires, werewolves, chainsaw maniacs) requiring weapon-specific strategies. Environmental variety increases complexity. Key-locked doors and keyed progression create deliberate pacing.
difficulty_curve.late_game: Pyramids, haunted castles, and alien-themed levels concentrate multiple monster types with higher density. Advanced weapons become essential. Dr. Tongue's castle provides final gauntlet. Developers noted 'really brutal level design' in latter stages.
notable_spikes: First appearance of chainsaw maniac (European: axeman lumberjack) - sudden mobility and damage threat
Introduction of day-to-night cycles - permanent loss of tourist neighbors to werewolf transformation
Pyramid levels - concentrated mummy and giant ant enemies
UFO/alien levels - introduces flying saucer enemies and squidmen
Dr. Tongue's castle finale - consolidation of all monster types and endgame boss encounter

notable_level_patterns.description: Open residential areas with scattered enemies, multiple neighbor types to rescue, weapon caches distributed across space. Establishes core rescue-and-clear loop. Low pressure introduction.
Specific locations host enemy types matching aesthetic (castle = chainsaw/axeman; pyramid = mummies/ants; mall = varied indoor threats). Weapon effectiveness hints tied to monster source material.
Daytime level with tourist neighbors gradually darkens. Upon nightfall, tourists transform into werewolves and become unrescuable. Creates hidden time limit and penalty for slow play.
notable_level_patterns.example_level: Later summer/outdoor levels (exact level numbers not documented)

notable_level_patterns.description: 7 optional bonus levels distributed among 48 core stages. Reward exploration and skill mastery; not required for progression but provide scoring opportunities and extra lives.
Dr. Tongue castle culmination. Consolidates mechanics and enemy variety into endgame challenge. Represents narrative payoff for entire progression.
